output:
I’m reviewing the implementation for story `21-1-extract-workflow-actors` as a code review, focusing on changed files, correctness, security, architecture fit, and whether the story’s acceptance criteria are actually covered.I’ve isolated the story commit and the story artifact. Next I’m reading the story requirements and the exact diff against the parent commit so I can review the implementation instead of the whole dirty worktree.I have the extraction diff. I’m checking the new module boundaries now, especially whether the new `workflow-types.ts` accidentally reintroduces the monolith as a dependency or drops any exported surface the rest of the code expects.The main thing standing out is the new `workflow-types.ts` dependency direction. I’m checking whether that creates a real architecture violation or a compatibility break, and whether any previously exported symbols were dropped during the extraction.The architecture doc is explicit enough that I want the exact component-boundary lines in front of me before I call this. I’m pulling those references and the current export surface from `workflow-machine.ts`.I have two concrete architecture/AC concerns already. I’m grabbing stable line numbers now so the review can point to the exact dependency edges and requirement mismatch, not just paraphrase them.1. High: the extraction still depends on the monolith through the new type layer. [workflow-types.ts:11](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-types.ts#L11) imports `EngineConfig` from [workflow-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-machine.ts), and [workflow-actors.ts:21](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L21) imports those types. That means `workflow-actors -> workflow-types -> workflow-machine`, which violates the story’s required dependency direction in [21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md:59](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md#L59), [21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md:117](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md#L117), and AD6’s “`workflow-types.ts` — shared types (no deps)” / “`workflow-actors.ts` — depends on types” contract in [architecture-xstate-engine.md:334](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture-xstate-engine.md#L334). It may compile because the import is type-only, but architecturally this story has not actually established the boundary it was supposed to.

2. Medium: AC coverage is giving a false sense of success for that boundary. The story verifies “no circular dependency” with `grep -c 'workflow-machine' src/lib/workflow-actors.ts` in [21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md:25](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/21-1-extract-workflow-actors.md#L25), but the actual back-edge now lives in [workflow-types.ts:11](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-types.ts#L11), so the current verification can pass while the architectural goal still fails. That leaves the story materially under-covered on the most important non-functional requirement it claims to satisfy.

Behaviorally, the implementation looks stable: the attached proof shows build, Vitest, lint, type-check, and line-budget checks passing. I did not find a separate security defect in this extraction. The failure is architectural and verification-related, not an obvious runtime regression.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>
1. `workflow-actors.ts` still depends on `workflow-machine.ts` indirectly via `workflow-types.ts` importing `EngineConfig`, violating the story’s required dependency direction and AD6 layering.
2. The story’s AC/proof for “no circular dependency” is insufficient: it only greps `workflow-actors.ts`, so it misses the new back-edge introduced in `workflow-types.ts`.
</issues>
